catalyst.event: Q2 2026 earnings release — first full quarter to reflect post-price-war margin stabilization and overseas mix shift; key tests are gross margin trajectory (vs. 17.2% TTM), monthly export run-rate, and any guidance on 5-min Super e-Platform vehicle ramp
catalyst.date_or_window: 2026-08-28 (±2 weeks, BYD historical Q2 reporting window)
catalyst.source: BYD H1 2026 interim report scheduled per HKEX/SZSE disclosure calendar

rightIf_conditions: Q2 2026 earnings (Aug 2026): GM ≥ 18.5% AND export volume ≥ 50K/month run-rate AND overseas revenue mix > 22% (vs ~15% in 2024)
FY2026 net income returns to >38B (above FY2024 trough comparable)
Forward P/E re-rates from 17.7x toward 21-22x as EPS estimates revised up by sell-side
wrongIf_conditions: Revenue growth <-10% for 2 consecutive quarters (per watchlist wrongIf)
Gross margin drops below 15% in any reported quarter — invalidates margin recovery mechanism
Q2 2026 export volume run-rate falls below 40K/month — overseas mix thesis broken
Net income YoY decline exceeds -40% in any quarter (per watchlist)
what_changes_our_mind: If H1 2026 export volume run-rate falls below 45K/month (vs current ~50K) for two consecutive months AND overseas gross margin compresses below domestic GM in interim disclosure, the export-mix thesis is broken — convert to consensus neutral stance.
variant_view_one_sentence: Market believes 5-min charging is the bull case → We believe overseas mix + premium brand ASP lift is the real driver → Mechanism is export run-rate scaling + Hungary/Brazil capacity unlock + Yangwang/Denza margin contribution becoming material in H2 2026.

expected_pnl_asymmetry.upside_if_right: +30% to +40% (toward 130-140 RMB) on margin+volume beat and EPS revision

quantification.metric_target: FY2026 gross margin and net income recovery
